title: Simulation generates VCD files with no timescale
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Simulate a Verilog file with `sim` and write output to a VCD file with `-vcd out.vcd`.

## Expected behavior

The generated VCD file should include a timescale, even just a placeholder, e.g. `$timescale 1ns $end`.

## Actual behavior

The VCD file does not contain any timescale. This confuses some viewers, such as [Scansion](http://www.logicpoet.com/scansion/).

A trivial workaround follows. This ignores any actual timescale defined by the file under simulation, but it does at least make Scansion happy:

```
diff --git a/passes/sat/sim.cc b/passes/sat/sim.cc
index fb496ff8..2e68f803 100644
--- a/passes/sat/sim.cc
+++ b/passes/sat/sim.cc
@@ -647,6 +647,7 @@ struct SimWorker : SimShared
 		int id = 1;
 		top->write_vcd_header(vcdfile, id);
 
+		vcdfile << stringf("$timescale 1ns $end\n");
 		vcdfile << stringf("$enddefinitions $end\n");
 	}
```
